hero.name: Carlos Castaneda
hero.eyebrow: 1925–1998 · anthropologist · author · Toltec sorcery · the luminous egg and the assemblage point
hero.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 25%, hsl(255 55% 50% / 0.55) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 80%, hsl(20 35% 22% / 0.7) 0%, transparent 60%), linear-gradient(180deg, hsl(255 45% 55%) 0%, hsl(285 30% 35%) 50%, hsl(20 30% 18%) 100%)
hero.welcome_md:
The Peruvian-born American anthropologist whose twelve-book series — beginning with *The Teachings of Don Juan* (1968) and ending with *The Active Side of Infinity* (1998) — carries the most operationally precise teaching the Coherence Network has inherited on **the assemblage point**: the locus on the luminous-egg energy body where perception assembles reality from the field of what could be perceived.

*Castaneda the man is contested. Castaneda the transmitter of a specific phenomenological vocabulary for shifting perception is, in this body's reading, load-bearing. Both are honoured here.*

facts.label: Lived
facts.value_md: 25 December 1925, Cajamarca, Peru — 27 April 1998, Los Angeles, California. PhD anthropology, UCLA (1973).

facts.label: The teachers in his books
facts.value_md: **don Juan Matus** (Yaqui sorcerer; primary teacher in the books) and **don Genaro Flores** (Mazatec, don Juan's ally). Whether they existed as described, as composites, or as literary devices remains contested. The teachings carried under their names landed in many lives regardless of that question.

facts.label: The twelve books
facts.value_md: *The Teachings of Don Juan* (1968) *A Separate Reality* (1971) *Journey to Ixtlan* (1972) *Tales of Power* (1974) — assemblage point named *The Second Ring of Power* (1977) *The Eagle's Gift* (1981) *The Fire from Within* (1984) — deepest treatment of the energy body and assemblage point *The Power of Silence* (1987) *The Art of Dreaming* (1993) *Magical Passes* (1998) — Tensegrity *The Wheel of Time* (1998) *The Active Side of Infinity* (1998) — posthumous

articles.body_md:
Castaneda's teachers describe the human energy body as a luminous egg containing all the energy fields that could be perceived. On this egg there is one specific point — the **assemblage point** — where energy threads are gathered and interpreted into a perceptual world. *Position determines reality.* The ordinary human position gives the ordinary human reality. The point can move — through dreaming, illness, ceremony, plant medicines, sustained inner work, or shock — and other equally vivid realities are perceived.

The sorcerer's discipline is not believing in alternative realities. It is moving the assemblage point at will, and underneath that, learning that one has been doing it unconsciously all along. Every reaction, every certainty, every *that's just how it is* was already an assemblage choice made too fast to see. This is the operational core of the body's [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) concept.
articles.heading: The assemblage point as teaching

articles.body_md:
- The assemblage-point vocabulary → [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) (the seed concept in this body from his lineage).
- The luminous-egg energy body → [lc-perception-as-interface](/vision/lc-perception-as-interface) (Hoffman's frame is the empirical sibling).
- Confirmed by [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba) 's essay *On Frequency, Consciousness and the Assemblage Point* (2026-05-11), which weaves Castaneda's teaching with the gunas, the chakras, and Socrates' *Gorgias* as one continuous map of consciousness.
- The discipline of *moving the point at will* → resonant with [lc-coherence-over-control](/vision/lc-coherence-over-control) and [lc-presence-over-protection](/vision/lc-presence-over-protection).
articles.heading: What Castaneda has given the Coherence Network

articles.body_md: Anthropological scholarship from the 1970s onward questioned whether don Juan Matus existed as a single historical Yaqui sorcerer; whether the books are literal fieldwork, composites, or literary creation remains contested. Separately, allegations of grave harms within Castaneda's late inner circle (the "witches") have surfaced in journalism and memoir; some former members went missing after his death. This body holds the teaching of the assemblage point as load-bearing while taking the human allegations seriously. The phenomenology a body can test in its own perception does not require Castaneda to have been a trustworthy or harmless person; the history of teachers is full of harmful messengers carrying real maps. Readers are invited to walk further into both the teaching and the criticism with their own discernment.
articles.heading: On the controversies

metadata.title: Carlos Castaneda — the assemblage-point lineage source · Toltec sorcery in books | Coherence Network
metadata.description: A welcome to Carlos Castaneda (1925–1998) — anthropologist and author whose Toltec-sorcery books (Don Juan, Don Genaro) carry the assemblage-point teaching that grounds lc-assemblage-point in this body's vocabulary.

en.footer_md:
**Public anchors:** [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carlos_Castaneda) · [castaneda.com (Cleargreen)](https://castaneda.com/)

**In-body record:** [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) · [lc-perception-as-interface](/vision/lc-perception-as-interface) · [lc-coherence-over-control](/vision/lc-coherence-over-control) · [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba) (the in-body teacher who reads Castaneda alongside the chakra map)

*Held honestly. The teaching of the assemblage point is load-bearing in this body's vocabulary; the human history around Castaneda himself is contested. Readers are invited to walk further into both with their own discernment.*

note_from_body.body_md: Castaneda is one of the contested figures of twentieth-century spiritual literature. Anthropological scholarship has questioned the literal-fact basis of his books; allegations from members of his late inner circle (the "witches") have surfaced grave harms. Holding both: the teaching of the assemblage point has been operationally useful in many lives, including this network's. We hold the teaching honestly while not flattening the controversies into it.
